🗺️ Visual Reference
The Degree Progression
The path of a Freemason from Candidate to the Higher Degrees
✦  ✦  ✦
🕯️
First Degree — Entered Apprentice
The beginning of the Masonic journey. The candidate is initiated, receives the working tools of the EA, and is placed in the North-East corner as the foundation stone. He learns the three Grand Principles: Brotherly Love, Relief, and Truth.
Initiation24-inch GaugeCommon GavelChisel
🌗
Second Degree — Fellowcraft
The Mason is passed to the Second Degree. He ascends the winding staircase to the Middle Chamber, studies the liberal arts and sciences, and learns the significance of the letter G — Geometry and the Grand Geometrician.
PassingSquareLevelPlumb RuleShibboleth
⭐
Third Degree — Master Mason
The sublime degree. The Mason is raised through the legend of Hiram Abiff, contemplates mortality and immortality, and receives the substituted secrets. He is now a full member of the Craft.
RaisingSkirretPencilCompassesMahabone
🏛️
Holy Royal Arch — Exaltation
Considered the completion of the Third Degree in English Masonry. Three sojourners discover the lost Sacred Word beneath the ruins of Solomon's Temple. Members become Royal Arch Companions.
ExaltationZerubbabelSacred Word Restored
🔨
Mark Master Mason — Advancement
Each Mason receives a personal mark. The degree teaches that the stone rejected by the builders becomes the headstone of the corner — a lesson in recognition and worth.
AdvancementThe KeystonePsalm 118:22
🌹
Rose Croix — 18th Degree
The first degree of the Ancient and Accepted Scottish Rite. Explicitly Christian in symbolism — the Pelican, the Resurrection, INRI. The ceremony moves from darkness and mourning to light and joy.
AASRChristianThe PelicanINRI
⚔️
Knights Templar — Preceptory
A chivalric Christian order. Members become Sir Knights. The Order of Malta is conferred at the same meeting. Governed by the Great Priory of England and Wales.
ChristianSir KnightOrder of Malta
✦ So Mote It Be ✦
The Lodge Room
Layout, orientation and positions of the officers
✦  ✦  ✦
Key to the Diagram
W.M. — Worshipful Master · sits in the East (place of light)
S.W. — Senior Warden · sits in the West (setting sun)
J.W. — Junior Warden · sits in the South (sun at meridian)
S.D. — Senior Deacon · carries messages from WM
J.D. — Junior Deacon · assists with candidates
I.G. — Inner Guard · admits on proof
TYL. — Tyler · guards outer door with sword
B / J — Pillars Boaz (left) and Jachin (right)
V.S.L. — Volume of Sacred Law on the Altar
✦ So Mote It Be ✦
The Working Tools
Three degrees, nine tools — each a lesson for life
✦  ✦  ✦
FIRST DEGREE 🕯️
📏
24-Inch Gauge
Entered Apprentice
Divides the day into thirds — prayer, labour, and rest. Teaches the wise use of time.
🔨
Common Gavel
Entered Apprentice
The force of conscience — knocks off all vain and unbecoming thoughts from the mind.
⚒️
The Chisel
Entered Apprentice
Points out the advantages of education — prepares us to be fit members of well-organised society.
SECOND DEGREE 🌗
📐
The Square
Fellowcraft
To regulate our lives and actions — morality and integrity in all our dealings.
⚖️
The Level
Fellowcraft
Equality — we are all partakers of the same nature. Death is the grand leveller.
🪜
The Plumb Rule
Fellowcraft
Justness and uprightness of life and actions — the criterion of moral rectitude and truth.
THIRD DEGREE ⭐
🪡
The Skirret
Master Mason
Points out the straight and undeviating line of conduct laid down in the Volume of the Sacred Law.
✏️
The Pencil
Master Mason
Our words and actions are observed and recorded by the Almighty Architect of the Universe.
🧭
The Compasses
Master Mason
The unerring justice of God — determines calculations and reminds us of our final accounting.
✦ So Mote It Be ✦
The Three Pillars
Wisdom, Strength, and Beauty — the foundation of every well-governed Lodge
✦  ✦  ✦
🦉
WISDOM
Ionic Order
To contrive
🦁
STRENGTH
Doric Order
To support
🌸
BEAUTY
Corinthian Order
To adorn
🦉 Wisdom — King Solomon
Represented by the Worshipful Master in the East, and by the Ionic column. Wisdom directs the Lodge — nothing should be undertaken without wisdom to contrive it rightly. The WM sits in the East because wisdom, like the sun, rises there.
🦁 Strength — Hiram King of Tyre
Represented by the Senior Warden in the West, and by the Doric column — the oldest and most massive order. Strength supports the work once wisdom has conceived it. The SW marks the setting sun and closes what the day has built.
🌸 Beauty — Hiram Abiff
Represented by the Junior Warden in the South, and by the Corinthian column — the most ornate order. Beauty adorns and perfects. The JW marks the sun at its meridian — the height of beauty and light in the day.
The Three Lesser Lights
Three candles situated East, South, and West represent the Sun (to rule the day), the Moon (to govern the night), and the Master of the Lodge (to rule and direct his Lodge). The North has no Lesser Light — it is a place of darkness.
The Three Great Lights
The Volume of the Sacred Law — governs our faith. The Square — regulates our actions. The Compasses — keeps us in due bounds with all mankind.
✦ So Mote It Be ✦
Officer Progression
The traditional path through the offices of the Lodge
✦  ✦  ✦
🥂 Steward
Assists at the Festive Board. Learns the toasts, the harmonies, and the procedures of the Lodge meeting. The first step on the progressive ladder.
No fixed seat in the Lodge room
🗡️ Inner Guard
Admits Masons on proof and receives candidates on the point of a sharp instrument. First officer encountered inside the door. Guards the inner door under command of the Junior Warden.
Inside the door, South
🪄 Junior Deacon
Prepares and presents candidates for initiation. Monitors entry and exit. Carries messages from the Senior Warden to the Junior Warden and throughout the Lodge.
Right of the Senior Warden, in the West
🔱 Senior Deacon
Introduces and accompanies visiting Masons. Carries messages from the Worshipful Master to the Senior Warden. A busy and visible role in the ceremony.
Right of the Worshipful Master, in the East
☀️ Junior Warden
Marks the sun at its meridian — High Noon. Oversees the Craft during the hours of refreshment. Arranges Lodge business with visitors.
In the South
🌙 Senior Warden
Second in command. Marks the setting sun and closes the Lodge. Pays the workmen their wages in the Master's absence. Must be ready to assume the Master's chair.
In the West
👑 Worshipful Master
Rules and directs the Lodge. Opens and closes all three degrees. Confers the degrees upon candidates. Sits in the East — the place of light. The highest office in the Lodge.
In the East
🏛️ Immediate Past Master
Supports the new Master. A source of experience and wisdom for the Lodge. May be considered for the Royal Arch and higher degrees.
To the left of the Worshipful Master
Appointed Officers
Chaplain — leads in prayer, custodian of the VSL
Treasurer — maintains exact and honest financial records
Secretary — handles all correspondence and the roll of members
Almoner — alerts members to those needing assistance
Charity Steward — coordinates charitable giving
Tyler — guards the outer door with a drawn sword
✦ So Mote It Be ✦
The Five Points of Fellowship
The obligations of brotherly care given in the position of the Third Degree
✦  ✦  ✦
1
Hand to Hand
That the hand given to a Mason shall be the sure pledge of Brotherhood — a promise of trust, reliability, and fraternal bond that cannot be broken.
2
Foot to Foot
That the foot shall traverse through danger and difficulties to assist a deserving Brother — no journey is too far for a Brother in need.
3
Knee to Knee
That the posture of daily supplications shall remind us of a Brother's wants — to pray for him, to remember him in our devotions, and to seek help on his behalf.
4
Breast to Breast
That the breast shall be the sacred repository of a Brother's secrets — when entrusted, they are locked within and will never be revealed.
5
Hand over Back
That the hand shall support a Brother from falling — to defend his character when unjustly attacked, and to support his reputation as we would our own.
The Substituted Word
The word Mahabone — meaning "the brother is smitten" — is given only in the position of the Five Points of Fellowship. It was substituted by King Solomon when the true secrets could not be recovered after the death of Hiram Abiff. The word is lettered or given in a low breath except in open Lodge.
✦ So Mote It Be ✦
Solomon's Temple
The central symbol of Freemasonry and its key features
✦  ✦  ✦
👑
King Solomon
Commissioned the Temple around 960 BC. The wisest of kings, he directed its construction over seven years. Represents Wisdom — the first pillar of the Lodge.
⚓
Hiram King of Tyre
Provided the cedar and fir timber from Lebanon and sent skilled craftsmen to Solomon. Represents Strength — the resources and support that make great works possible.
🔨
Hiram Abiff
The Widow's Son — the chief architect and superintendent of the works. Murdered before the Temple's completion. His legend forms the heart of the Third Degree. Represents Beauty.
🏛️
The Twin Pillars — Boaz & Jachin
Boaz (left/North) — "In Strength". Jachin (right/South) — "To Establish". When conjoined they signify Stability. Made of molten brass, 17½ cubits high, surmounted by celestial and terrestrial globes.
🌟
The Sanctum Sanctorum
The Holy of Holies — the innermost chamber where the Ark of the Covenant was kept. Entered only by the High Priest once a year. In Royal Arch Masonry, the vault beneath its foundations conceals the lost Sacred Word.
🌊
The Brazen Sea
A great bronze basin supported by twelve oxen — used by the priests for ritual purification. Symbolic of cleansing and preparation before entering the sacred presence.
🌿
The Sprig of Acacia
Found blooming at the head of Hiram Abiff's grave. The evergreen acacia is a symbol of the immortality of the soul — it blooms even in death, and so shall we rise beyond the grave.
✦ So Mote It Be ✦
Masonic Symbols
The tracing boards and key symbols across the three degrees
✦  ✦  ✦
FIRST DEGREE
🔲
Chequered Floor
Light and darkness — the profane world vs the light of initiation. Good and evil.
FIRST DEGREE
🪨
Rough Ashlar
The EA in his natural state — unpolished, unrefined. The starting point of all Masonic work.
FIRST DEGREE
🟦
Perfect Ashlar
The Fellowcraft — shaped, polished and ready. The goal of Masonic self-improvement.
FIRST DEGREE
🌅
The East
Where the sun rises — the place of light. The WM sits East, the source of all Masonic instruction.
SECOND DEGREE
🔡
The Letter G
Geometry — the first science. And God, the Grand Geometrician of the Universe, to whom all must submit.
SECOND DEGREE
🌽
Shibboleth
The password of the Fellowcraft — depicted by an ear of corn near a fall of water. Denotes Plenty.
THIRD DEGREE
⏳
The Hourglass
Time passes swiftly. Our lives run out as the sand falls — value each moment and use it wisely.
THIRD DEGREE
🌿
Sprig of Acacia
Immortality of the soul. Bloomed at Hiram's grave — reminder that though the body dies, the soul endures.
THIRD DEGREE
☠️
Skull & Crossbones
Emblems of mortality — placed before the candidate to remind him of his inevitable destiny.
ALL DEGREES
🔺
The All-Seeing Eye
God observes all our thoughts and actions. Nothing is hidden from the Great Architect of the Universe.
ALL DEGREES
⚓
The Anchor & Ark
Hope and a well-grounded hope — the anchor steadies us through the storms of life.
ALL DEGREES
☀️
The Point in Circle
A Mason cannot err while bounded by this circle — representing the limits of good conduct.
✦ So Mote It Be ✦
